headline: Gilead's Breast Cancer Drug Trodelvy Wins FDA Nod for Expanded Use
summary: GILD wins FDA approval to expand Trodelvy into first-line metastatic triple-negative breast cancer, opening a broader treatment opportunity.

raw.headline: Gilead's Breast Cancer Drug Trodelvy Wins FDA Nod for Expanded Use
raw.summary: GILD wins FDA approval to expand Trodelvy into first-line metastatic triple-negative breast cancer, opening a broader treatment opportunity.

headline: Gilead's Trodelvy Expands Reach With New FDA Approval In Breast Cancer
summary: FDA approves Gilead's Trodelvy for first-line metastatic triple-negative breast cancer, expanding use alone and with Keytruda.

raw.headline: Gilead's Trodelvy Expands Reach With New FDA Approval In Breast Cancer
raw.summary: FDA approves Gilead's Trodelvy for first-line metastatic triple-negative breast cancer, expanding use alone and with Keytruda.

headline: FDA Approves KEYTRUDA® (pembrolizumab) and KEYTRUDA QLEX™ (pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph), each with Trodelvy® (sacituzumab govitecan-hziy) as First-Line Treatment of PD-L1+ (CPS ≥10) Advanced Triple-Negative Breast Cancer (TNBC)
summary: RAHWAY, N.J., June 25, 2026--Merck (NYSE: MRK), known as MSD outside of the United States and Canada, today announced the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approved KEYTRUDA® (pembrolizumab) and KEYTRUDA QLEX™ (pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph), each in combination with Trodelvy® (sacituzumab govitecan-hziy), Gilead’s Trop-2-directed antibody-drug conjugate (ADC), for the first-line treatment of adult patients with unresectable locally advanced or metastatic triple-negative br
